intent: A highly granular todo-management platform engineered for extreme scalability and domain separation, utilizing a micro-fragmented database schema where each task attribute, status, and dependency exists in a unique, isolated table. The system exposes a massive API surface area, providing dedicated granular endpoints for every CRUD operation on specific data shards to facilitate high-concurrency enterprise auditing and ultra-precise observability.

entities.name: TaskLifecycleState
entities.description: An isolated ledger tracking the granular state transitions of a task, mapped via a surrogate key to prevent table contention.

entities.name: DependencyGraphNode
entities.description: A dedicated structural table mapping parent-child relationships and blocking constraints between tasks using adjacency list patterns for recursive query optimization.

entities.name: AttributeValueSnapshot
entities.description: An immutable, versioned store for task-specific metadata fields, utilizing a key-value relational mapping to support sparse data schema flexibility.

entities.name: AuditTrailFragment
entities.description: A high-frequency append-only table storing immutable operation logs for every granular API interaction to satisfy enterprise compliance requirements.

entities.name: ConcurrencySemaphore
entities.description: A specialized locking entity that manages distributed task-level access control and race condition prevention across multiple micro-services.

entities.name: ExecutionTelemetryPoint
entities.description: A high-performance storage entity capturing micro-second latency metrics and throughput data for specific API shards to feed the observability engine.

entities.name: AccessPolicyMapping
entities.description: A granular permission store that dictates fine-grained CRUD access at the individual attribute level, decoupled from broader user entities.

architecture.description: The system utilizes a service-mesh mediated architecture where incoming requests are validated against the AccessPolicyMapping before being routed to a micro-service domain. Data ingestion occurs through granular API endpoints that trigger localized ACID transactions across the fragmented schema. To maintain consistency across the 500+ tables, a distributed transaction orchestrator utilizes the ConcurrencySemaphore to lock relevant graph nodes. Post-mutation, the system emits events to an asynchronous bus, which populates the AuditTrailFragment and ExecutionTelemetryPoint, ensuring non-blocking operations and maintaining latency targets. Data retrieval is optimized via read-replicas and high-performance indexing on surrogate keys, allowing the system to reconstruct task context in real-time from the fragmented AttributeValueSnapshot and TaskLifecycleState sources.

endpoints.summary: Create a new task state transition
Create a new dependency node relationship
validation_rules: parent_id must refer to existing node_id

endpoints.summary: Retrieve the latest version of task attribute snapshots
Append a new audit trail fragment for operations
Acquire a distributed semaphore lock for a task

components.content: List of task lifecycle transitions and current statuses
Trigger new task lifecycle state transition
Visual representation of task relationship nodes
Execution latency metrics based on telemetry points

rules.description: Ensure strict state transition integrity within TaskLifecycleState
rules.logic: A transition to a target state is only permitted if the current state exists in the allowed_transitions map for the specific task surrogate key.
Before inserting a new parent-child relationship, perform a recursive query to verify that the prospective parent is not a descendant of the child.
Every mutation event must verify that the AuditTrailFragment sequence ID increments by exactly one, preventing gaps or unauthorized modification of the log history.
No ACID transaction may proceed to modify AttributeValueSnapshot or TaskLifecycleState without an active, non-expired lock handle from ConcurrencySemaphore for the specific surrogate key.
The request context must contain a verifiable identity token that matches an entry in AccessPolicyMapping granting specific CRUD permissions for the target attribute key before record mutation.
Updates to AttributeValueSnapshot must strictly increment the version number and write a new row rather than performing an in-place update to maintain immutability.
